articles.headline: Why are there hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, and why has FIFA been criticised?
articles.description: All 104 matches at the 2026 World Cup are stopping for two hydration breaks. Why have they been stipulated by FIFA, and why have they been criticised?

images.name:
Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.alt: Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium

images.name: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium [1296x729]
images.caption: Argentina's Enzo Fernández said he felt "dizzy" while playing in "very dangerous" temperatures during last summer's Club World Cup in the United States.
images.alt: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium

images.name:
Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Advertising is prominent on stadium screens during drinks breaks at the World Cup.
images.alt: Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium

images.name:
Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Virgil van Dijk has been critical of the hydration breaks introduced at this summer's World Cup.
images.alt: Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium

images.name:
The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti was thankful for the opportunity to speak to his players when Brazil found themselves a goal behind to Morocco in the first half of their World Cup opener.
images.alt: The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium

images.name:
Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Hydration breaks provide a window for coaches to gather their players and tweak their tactics.
images.alt: Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia

images.name:
Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Drinks breaks could have a major effect on the outcome of matches at the World Cup.
images.alt: Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
images.credit: Kirill KUDRYAVTSEV / AFP via Getty Images

commentary.text: Nemanja Vidic says there is nothing to fear from Germany, but then what does he fear?
Join us at 1130 GMT as Germany look to continue their good start against Serbia.
Good afternoon all and welcome along to live coverage of Germany's game with Serbia from Group D. Can the Germans continue their excellent form from the first game, when they thumped Australia 4-0?

Unsurprisingly, Joachim Low sends out the same eleven players that made Australia look like a bunch of staggering drunks in their opening game. The main man in that game was Mesut Ozil, and he'll be the focal point again today.

Serbia suffered a disappointing defeat at the hands of Ghana in their opening game, and really need a win here. They make three changes: Lukovic, Pantelic and Milijas go out, Subotic in, Kuzmanovic and Ninkovic come in.
Why not have a look at Germany legend Jurgen Klinsmann chatting about that 4-0 win over Australia while we wait for kick-off?

I'm looking forward to seeing Krasic and Kuzmanovic for Serbia. In my most recent Football Manager game, Kuzmanovic is the boss in my Champions League winning Newcastle side. Hey, we can all dream can't we?
As for Germany, Miroslav Klose is now just four goals behind the World Cup's all-time leading scorer, Ronaldo. He should have a good few more opportunities in this game I'll wager.
The teams are out into beautiful sunshine in Port Elizabeth, Its about 19 degrees and we're currently enjoying the Serbian national anthem.
We're off...
An early ball into Germany's box but Zigic's header is weak and Neuer claims. Then Kuzmanovic tries a shot from range but it dribbles wide.
ESPN Stats & Info: Following his goal against Australia, Germany's Miroslav Klose (11 goals) is now only four behind Ronaldo's all-time record of 15 World Cup goals.
Such a good stat we produced it twice.
Ozil the architect again with a great chipped ball into the box but Khedira's volley is off target.
Good challenge from Subotic as Muller tries to cross from the right flank.
Lahm looks for Ozil but the Germany captain overhits the ball. Both teams are set up to attack here which is promising.
A rasping volley from Podolski flashes just wide of the post after a really good German break. He caught it flush but it was always spinning away from goal.
Klose is clumsy and late on Ivanovic and concedes a free-kick.
Germany are enjoying plenty of unchecked possession in the middle of the park. You fear for Serbia unless they get tighter and close down quicker.
Vidic does well as Podolski charges forward. The white shirts are getting plenty of time to pick out a pass.
First half chance for Serbia as Krasic gets one-on-one with Badstuber. He crosses to the back stick where Ninkovic blazes over on the volley. Better.
Klose has been booked for a clip on Ivanovic. That is half for that challenge and half for the one about five minutes ago. Clumsy.
Klose is invloved positively now as he looks to link with Ozil. The referee awards a goal kick and is proved right by the replay.
ESPN Stats & Info: Serbia have lost all four of their matches at the World Cup to date.
Good link-up between Ivanovic and Krasic and the latter draws a foul from Badstuber. Free-kick wide on the right. Aim for Zigic is my advice. He's massive.
Jovanovic clips one deep but Vidic can't get a head on it. Bit of a waste in truth.
Klose is a yard offside as Muller looked for him over the top. Very nearly.
. Nice turn from Kuzmanovic who weaves his way to the edge of the area but he can't find Jovanovic. Then Ivanovic brings down Podolski and is carded. Very soft
The referee is going card crazy here. Now Kolarov is booked for sliding in on Muller.
A good spell for Serbia but Germany win a corner now as Ivanovic nods behind.
Subotic heads away at the near post as Ozil delivers.
Now Khedira is carded for a tug on Krasic. This game is going to end with seven players against seven at this rate. Poor refereeing.
Bastian Schweinsteiger battles for the ball.
ESPN Stats & Info: Germany have conceded seven goals in their last three World Cup matches against teams from the Balkans (1-2 vs Bulgaria in 1994, 2-2 vs Yugoslavia and 0-3 vs Croatia in 1998).
A cracking run from Ninkovic is ruined when Zigic can't return the ball. Poor.
A long ball from Kolarov is too strong for Krasic and he can't keep it in play.
Germany's Miroslav Klose in action.
Great crossing opportunity for Muller but he's absolutely Mullered it over everyone's head. Muller by name, Muller by nature.
Deja vu. Muller's Mullered another cross right out of play on the opposite side. Not even three Zigic's on top of each other could have reached that one.
Nearly a nice ball from Schweinsteiger but Vidic cuts it out.
Then Klose turns the ball into the net but he was offside from Khedira's knock. Just about right too.
A poor five minutes for Muller continues with his toe-poked pass to Klose which is about 20 yards too strong. The boy's got kryptonite in his boots by the looks of it.
Lahm gets a deserved booking after hacking down Krasic. Zigic won the header and Krasic was first to the loose ball. Free-kick, 25 yards out.
Kolarov beat the wall with his effort but it was two yards wide of the near post. Not a bad effort.
Great defending from Vidic who gets a foot in as Klose tried to turn in the area.
Danger in Serbia's box as Klose nods down Muller's cross but Subotic hacks away. Pressure still on....
Klose has been sent off, for the two most innocuous fouls you will ever see. Pathetic refereeing. Just awful, awful, awful.
Referee Alberto Undiano Mallenco from Spain, is a joke. That was absolutely pathetic. He's ruined the game with those ridiculous bookings.
Serbia score! Zigic knocks down a cross and Jovanovic volleys home from five yards. Then he jumps into the crowd to celebrate. Joy unconfined
How things can turn. Germany were looking in no trouble at all, then their star striker is sent off for nothing and within two minutes they go behind.
Germany are ragged here. That red card has rocked them. Zigic did well to meet Krasic's cross and Jovanovic was cool under pressure.
Muller wins a corner for shellshocked Germany.
Great work from Podolski. Seconds after driving in a shot on the edge of the box he is back in his own half pinching the ball off Krasic.
ESPN Stats & Info: Germany's last red card at the World Cup was in 2002 against Cameroon in the group stage when Carsten Ramelow was sent off.
Pathetic from Stojkovic, as Schweinsteiger gives him a little bump. The 'keeper goes down looking to get his opponent booked but thankfully the referee keeps his cards in his pocket for once.
Muller does really well to win a corner after some trickery in the box. One minute added...

Khedira has hit the bar! The corner fell to him and he's whacked it off the crossbar, then Muller's overhead kick is cleared off the line. The ref has blown for a foul for the overhead kick. Sums up his half, which comes to a close now.

Well, an entertaining half that was overshadowed by a truly shocking red card for Miroslav Klose. Two innocuouos fouls and he gets his marching orders. The first was an inadvertent clip, the second when he tried to poke the ball away from Stankovic.
Next up, at 2pm, is the clash between the USA and Slovenia. Have a read of our preview here.
And after that, England take on Algeria in Cape Town. Here's our preview for that one.
Milan Jovanovic scores for Serbia.
We begin again...
First opening of the half to Germany as Podolski smashes a shot across the face of goal from a tight angle.
Ozil has taken the lone striker's role for Germany, with Podolski and Muller in support.
So, how should Serbia play this? Push forward and look for a second to kill off the Germans or sit back and hit them on the break?
Stankovic knocks a quality ball through for Ninkovic but Lahm was wide awake and knocked it back to Neuer.
Kuzmanovic picks out the right pass but overhits it as Krasic scampered down the right flank.
Milan Jovanovic celebrates his goal.
Lahm's slide rule pass finds Schweinsteiger but Vidic's tackle is a good one. There's plenty of life in this German side yet.
Ooh, that must have hurt. Schweinsteiger forces Stojkovic into a save before Khedira volleys Kolarov in the head. He's up already though. Hard as nails.
Joachim Low goes nuts as Lahm pinches the ball off Ninkovic but is penalised. Its fair to say this ref is not welcome in Germany any time soon.

ESPN Stats & Info: Jovanovic's goal for Serbia was the fifth time during the tournament that the first shot on target of the game for either team has been a goal: England (Gerrard v. USA), South Korea (Lee Jung-Soo v. Greece), South Africa (Tshabalala v. Mexico) and Paraguay (Alcaraz v. Italy) were the previous four.
Now Lahm is tripped on the edge of the box but the referee denies him a free-kick. Tension rising.
Another yellow, this time for Subotic as he lunges in on Ozil. The ref probably got that one right, actually.
A great chance for Podolski to equalise. He gets into the left channel and collects Khedira's ball before stroking the ball just wide of the post.
Another chance for Podolski as he is found by a cute touch from Ozil but he blasts into the side-netting.
PENALTY TO GERMANY
Nemanja Vidic has handled a cross needlessly and Podolski stands over the penalty.
SAVED! Stojkovic gets Vidic out of jail by saving Podolski's weak penalty and Serbia survive!

Well that will knock extra stuffing out of Germany. Vidic dived full length and brushed Khedira's cross with his hand, in a complete rush of blood, but Podolski can't convert. Vidic was booked for his trouble too.
Can Germany recover from another setback and get back into the game? They deserve something from their second half performance.
Serbia need to start pushing up the field and make their extra man count. They're playing as if it is they who are down to ten men.
Podolski gets away from Ivanovic again but lacks numbers in the box.
Jovanovic breaks upfield but his cross is snaffled by Neuer.
Serbia have hiot the post! A rare break upfield sees Krasic get into the area, feed Jovanovic, and his curling effort cannons back off the post. Great effort.
Germany about to make a double substitution it looks like. Cacau is stripped and ready.

Stojkovic gets a huge ovation as he scrambles to prevent a Germany corner. His penalty save was smart but hardly taxing. There will be a monument of him up in Belgrade before the full-time whistle I'll wager.
Podolski fires in another effort but this one is wayward.
Changes galore. Ninkovic is off and Kacar is on for Serbia. For Germany, Cacau and Marin are on for Ozil and Muller.
ESPN Stats & Info: Germany's last non-shootout penalty miss in the World Cup was by Uli Hoeness in 1974 versus Poland.
National hero Vladimir Stojkovic punches the ball.
Schweinsteiger is the latest to be booked after banging into Kolarov.
That is eight players booked and one red card now, and I can't recall a bad tackle.
Zigic has hit the bar! Krasic delivers a great cross and the big man towers high but powers his nod against the bar. Should have scored.
Rubbish from Kolarov after Jovanovic fed him in the box. He slices wide, horribly.
Kuzmanovic goes off and Petrovic comes on as Serbia make their second change.
Krasic wins a free-kick and spins it into the box but Petrovic's header is high and wide.
Gomez is on for Badstuber. An attacker for a defender.
Corner for Germany as Stojkovic fumbles a regulation shot. The corner is too long and no-one gets near it.
Jovanovic, the goalscorer, departs and Lazovic on.
ESPN Stats & Info: Five of the last seven goals Serbia have conceded at the World Cup have come in the last 15 minutes of games.
Good chance for Kacar as Zigic knocks down a cross into the box. He went for a side-footed volley but it was always rising.
Now the referee misses a blatant booking as Kacar piles in late on Cacau. The free-kick is nodded behind by Vdiic.
Vladimir Stojkovic makes a save from Podolski's penalty.
Podolski has another pop at goal but drags this one wide. It was from an angle but he has been wasteful today. Six efforts off target from him.
Germany still pouring forward. Serbia can pick them off on the counter if they play it right.
Just as I type that Zigic has a golden chance to play in Kacar but Mertesacker makes a vital block.
Terrible from Lazovic, as he overhits a simple ball for Kolarov who had run 45 yards up in support.
What a mess from Serbia. They had four-on-four there but wasted it. No cohesion at all. Come on Germany, let's have an equaliser.
Marin has been awful since he came on. He's just kicked the ball out of play, much to the fury of the coach Joachim Low.
Now Lahm's cross is mishit and Stojkovic can claim. I wonder how his statue is coming on in Belgrade Square? Three minutes added...

This will blow Group D wide open if it stays like this. Australia will be back in with a shout if they beat Ghana. Ghana will be clear favourites to top the group if they collect another three points.
A melee on the edge of the box ends when the ref gives a foul to Serbia. Subotic flung himself in the way of a Khedira shot before that. Stankovic has cramp now. Not surprised, he's about 40.
As that foul was awarded, for nothing by the way, Joachim Low slammed his bottle of water into the ground. He's going to give the ref both barrels after this, and well he might.

The final whistle sounds and Serbia have claimed a famous win. A big assist must go to Senor Alberto Undiano Mallenco, but even after his ridiculous red card for Klose, Podolski still missed a penalty .
Hot off the Soccernet press here is our match report.
Apparently the referee handed out 11 red cards in 17 La Liga matches last season. Hmm.
Stay tuned for some post-match reaction, which is bound to be suitably poisonous as far as Germany are concerned. Next up is Slovenia v USA in Group C.
I'll be back in five minutes or so to launch our live coverage of Slovenia v USA. The gamecast is here.
